publication.showAs: OJ-9-2024-03-13 — Agenda (Order of business) for the EP plenary, Strasbourg, Wednesday 13 March 2024
publication.name: ep-oj-agenda

eventRef.showAs: Agenda for the week (Monday 11 to Thursday 14 March 2024) adopted by the plenary on Monday 11 March 2024
Wednesday 13 March 2024 voting time, Strasbourg — AI Act first reading position vote scheduled at item N of the voting list
Voting time held at noon (Strasbourg time) — AI Act adopted 523/46/49

TLCOrganization.showAs: EP Conference of Presidents (sets the draft agenda)

proprietary.akndiff:researchNotes:
Friction log for EU EP plenary agenda OJ-9-2024-03-13 — Phase 1.
1. EP AGENDA IS WEEK-LEVEL, NOT SESSION-LEVEL. The Conference of Presidents adopts the draft agenda for the entire week of plenary (Monday-Thursday in Strasbourg, or Wed-only in Brussels). Each day has its own published agenda OJ. We model 13 March 2024 specifically. Spanish ES citation modeled a single Comisión session (DSCD-15-CO-482, 9-dic-2025); EU equivalent is one day of plenary, but the agenda is part of a weekly bundle. CitationTable.scheduledFor takes a single timestamp, fits both.
2. AGENDA ITEMS REFERENCE BILLS BY THEIR PROCEDURE FILE. The agenda item for the AI Act vote references it as "2021/0106(COD) — A9-0188/2023 — Brando Benifei / Dragoş Tudorache". Schema's citation type carries agenda items inside body XML (no side table). Same as ES (agenda items live in body). No new schema friction beyond what was noted for ES.
3. CITATION TYPE NOT IN AKN CORE. The akndiff:citation extension was Spain's research artifact — interop with AKN-native tools would not recognize it. Same friction as ES. Cross-country confirmed.
4. NO CONVENING DECISION SEPARATE FROM AGENDA. Spain has a specific "convocation" act (the Mesa decision). EU just has the Conference of Presidents adopting the draft agenda by internal procedure — no formal convening document beyond the agenda itself. CitationTable.convenedBody captures the plenary, but the convoking authority (CoP) is different from the convened body (EP plenary). Same nuance as ES.
5. MULTILINGUAL OFFICIAL VERSIONS. Like all EP documents, the agenda is published simultaneously in 24 official languages. Schema language=eng. Multilingual stretch confirmed (cross-doc-type pattern).
6. AGENDA ADOPTED 2 DAYS BEFORE THE SESSION. The agenda for the week is adopted on Monday by plenary; we model it as published on 2024-03-11 (Monday) but scheduled for 2024-03-13 (Wednesday). Spanish citation also has a publication-date/session-date distinction (BOCG vs DSCD-CO). Cross-country confirmed.
7. NO SESSION OUTCOME ON THE AGENDA ITSELF. The agenda lists what WAS scheduled, not what happened. Outcomes (vote results, postponements) live in P9_PV (minutes) and P9_TA (adopted texts). ES citation also doesn't capture vote outcome. Same gap cross-country.

longTitle.p: EP plenary agenda (Order of business) — Strasbourg, Wednesday 13 March 2024 — OJ-9-2024-03-13. Voting time at noon. Agenda includes the vote on the EP first reading position on the AI Act (procedure 2021/0106(COD), report A9-0188/2023, co-rapporteurs Brando Benifei and Dragoş Tudorache).

body.p: The agenda for Wednesday 13 March 2024 voting time (12:00, Strasbourg) included multiple items. The AI Act vote was item P9_TA(2024)0138 in the voting list — the EP first reading position on the proposal for a regulation laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence (COM(2021)0206 – C9-0146/2021 – 2021/0106(COD)). The vote was preceded by the substantive debate held the previous afternoon (CRE-9-2024-03-12 ITM-006).

The AI Act vote was conducted by RECORDED VOTE (electronic, with each MEP's individual vote recorded). Result: 523 in favour / 46 against / 49 abstentions. Position adopted; transmitted to the Council for first reading consideration.
Other agenda items at the same voting time included files on consumer credit, packaging waste, soil monitoring, and a non-AI-related Council recommendation. Full agenda in source OJ-9-2024-03-13.
